title: Path Traversal
description: Using ../ sequences to access files outside the intended directory on a web server.
fullExplanation: Path traversal (directory traversal) exploits insufficient input validation to access files outside the web root. By injecting ../ sequences into file path parameters, attackers can read sensitive files like /etc/passwd, private keys, config files with credentials, or application source code. Also called dot-dot-slash attack.
keyFacts: CVE-2021-41773: Apache HTTP Server path traversal — actively exploited in the wild
Zip Slip: malicious zip with ../ in filenames — extract outside target directory
Canonicalize paths before validation — resolve symlinks and ../ first
Chroot jail / container isolation limits damage even if exploited
Log4Shell used JNDI path traversal for RCE
Windows: both / and \ work as path separators — test both

defenses.name: Input Validation
defenses.description: Reject ../, ..\ and encoded variants in file path parameters

defenses.name: Canonicalization
defenses.description: Resolve full path, verify it starts with allowed base directory

defenses.name: Chroot / Containers
defenses.description: Isolate application filesystem — limit accessible files

defenses.name: Least Privilege
defenses.description: Web server runs as low-privilege user — can't read sensitive files
